codeReview.summary: Solid implementation across all four deliverables. Migration 004 correctly maps reminded→in_progress (with reminded_at=updated_at) and archived→canceled using idempotent WHERE clauses. Migration 005 widens the CHECK constraint to allow 'cleanup_deleted', then soft-deletes [PT]-polluted todos and near-duplicate todos (same user_id+task within 5 seconds, keeping earliest). Both migrations are idempotent and reversible. The verify-migration.ts script queries all 8 status values and flags legacy/polluted rows. The 39 Vitest tests cover SQL structure validation for idempotency, mapping correctness, soft-delete patterns, cross-migration consistency, and all three VAL-MIGRATE assertions. Tests are static SQL analysis (string/regex matching on file contents) rather than runtime execution, which is a reasonable approach given the project has no local database — the cloud Supabase instance is the only target.

issues.description: Feature description says 'raw_message contains [PT]' but implementation uses 'task LIKE %[PT]%'. This is actually correct because polluted rows are todos where agent messages were incorrectly processed as inbound — the [PT] text ends up in the 'task' column of the todos table, not in message_logs.raw_message. However, the description-to-implementation discrepancy could cause confusion for future maintainers. A SQL comment clarifying this mapping would help.

The duplicate detection self-join (same user_id + task + within 5 seconds) could theoretically mark legitimate rapid-fire todos with identical text. This is extremely unlikely in a personal todo app, and the soft-delete makes it reversible. Acceptable trade-off, but worth noting.

All 39 tests validate SQL file content via string matching and regex, not by executing SQL against a database. This means SQL syntax errors or runtime behavior bugs (e.g., self-join edge cases) would not be caught. This is a known limitation given the project architecture (no local DB), not a defect.

The script makes 8 sequential queries (one per status value) plus 2 additional queries. A single 'SELECT status, count(*) FROM todos GROUP BY status' would be more efficient. Acceptable for a diagnostic script that runs infrequently.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker attempted to invoke 'backend-worker' skill but it was not found. The feature's skillName is 'backend-worker' but the .factory/skills directory does not exist. Workers for the cleanup-stabilization milestone cannot use their assigned skill.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows: Skill 'backend-worker' returned 'not found' error. skillFeedback.deviations confirms this: 'The backend-worker skill returned not found error. Implemented the feature directly.'

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md documents status values as 'pending, in_progress, done, not_confirmed, canceled' but migration 005 introduces 'cleanup_deleted' as a new valid status value in the CHECK constraint. AGENTS.md should mention cleanup_deleted as a system-internal status marker.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md 'Status Model (authoritative)' section lists 5 statuses. supabase/migrations/005_cleanup_polluted.sql adds 'cleanup_deleted' to the CHECK constraint at line 21.

issues.description: Feature description says 'raw_message contains [PT]' but SQL correctly uses 'task LIKE %[PT]%' since polluted rows are in the todos table. A clarifying SQL comment would help.
Duplicate detection self-join could theoretically catch legitimate rapid identical todos, but soft-delete makes this reversible.
Tests are static SQL string analysis, not runtime execution. Known limitation given no local DB.
verify-migration.ts makes 8 sequential queries instead of a single GROUP BY. Acceptable for a diagnostic script.

summary: PASS. All four deliverables (004_backfill_status.sql, 005_cleanup_polluted.sql, verify-migration.ts, 39 Vitest tests) are well-implemented. Migration 004 correctly maps reminded→in_progress and archived→canceled with idempotent WHERE clauses. Migration 005 soft-deletes [PT]-polluted and near-duplicate todos using reversible 'cleanup_deleted' status. The verify-migration script reports status counts and flags remaining legacy/polluted data. Tests cover all three VAL-MIGRATE assertions (001: legacy mapping, 002: idempotency, 003: pollution cleanup) plus cross-migration consistency and historical data preservation. Four non-blocking observations noted: description/implementation wording discrepancy on raw_message vs task column, theoretical false-positive risk in duplicate detection, tests being static analysis only, and sequential queries in verify script. Two shared-state gaps identified: missing backend-worker skill for this milestone, and AGENTS.md not documenting the new cleanup_deleted status value.
